title: Corpus completo — 145 itens codificados (100%)
thesis: ICONOCRACY: Alegoria Feminina na Historia da Cultura Juridica (Sec. XIX-XX)

candidates.titulo: Allegory, Liberty Seated — Selo definitivo da República Argentina
candidates.justificativa: Figura feminina de Liberty sentada, braço esquerdo apoiado em escudo com brasão argentino, sol nascente sobre o oceano ao fundo. Série definitiva de 22 selos. Tiragem do 1c verde: 70.920.000.

candidates.titulo: Zeichnet 8. Kriegsanleihe
candidates.justificativa: Figura feminina clássica segura espada numa mão e balança na outra. De um lado da balança, uma pomba da paz (mais pesada); do outro, moedas. Alegoria de que a paz supera o dinheiro, mas dinheiro é nec

candidates.titulo: Allégorie de la Justice (Joseph Stallaert, Brussels City Hall)
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical painting of Justice in the Mayor's Cabinet of Brussels City Hall, by Joseph Stallaert. Part of a triptych with Charity and Foresight — three female allegorical virtues required of a mayor.

candidates.titulo: Palais de Justice de Bruxelles (Joseph Poelaert)
candidates.justificativa: The Brussels Palace of Justice — one of the largest courthouses ever built and a monument to 19th-century legal grandeur. The 'pompous staircase with sculptures attired in Roman robes' and allegorical

candidates.titulo: 5 Francs — 50 ans de Belgique: La Belgique avec Constitution et Lion
candidates.justificativa: Commemorative coin for 50th anniversary of Belgian independence. Reverse: personification of Belgium leaning on lion, holding Belgian Constitution. Congress Column to left, Brussels Palace of Justice

candidates.titulo: 20 Francs Belgian Congo — Allegory of Europe with Mace and African Woman with Ivory Tusk
candidates.justificativa: Woman as allegory of Europe/Belgium holding mace placed on anvil. African woman at her side holding elephant ivory tusk. Explicit colonial visual hierarchy: white allegorical figure clothed and armed,

candidates.titulo: Estátua feminina na abertura da amostra dos produtos do Rio Grande do Sul
candidates.justificativa: Black and white photograph showing a female statue displayed at the National Exhibition of 1908. Female allegorical figure representing progress and abundance at the Rio Grande do Sul exhibition pavil

candidates.titulo: Detalhe do monumento à República, Praça da República, Belém
candidates.justificativa: Close-up detail of female allegorical figure holding a flag, standing on a lion. Part of the Monument to the Republic in Praça da República, Belém. The female figure personifies the Brazilian Republic

candidates.titulo: Monumento à República, Praça da República, Belém (vista geral)
candidates.justificativa: Full view of Monument to the Republic with female allegorical figure atop a column in Praça da República, Belém. The towering female figure dominates the urban landscape, arms raised, embodying the Re

candidates.titulo: Jardim do Campo da Aclamação (Campo de Santana): estátuas do Outono e da Primavera
candidates.justificativa: Sepia photograph showing female allegorical statues representing the seasons of Autumn and Spring in Campo de Santana (formerly Campo da Aclamação), Rio de Janeiro. Female figures as seasonal allegori

candidates.titulo: Alegoria da República (Décio Villares)
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical painting of the Brazilian Republic by Décio Villares, the positivist artist who designed the blue disc on Brazil's national flag. The female figure wears a Phrygian cap inscribed with 'Ord

candidates.titulo: Alegoria da República (Carlos Chambelland)
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical painting commissioned for Brazil's centennial of independence (1922). Female figure in flowing white and green robes, arm raised, accompanied by an angel, with male figures holding manuscr

candidates.titulo: As Estátuas Fallando (The Statues Speaking) — Justiça
candidates.justificativa: Article titled 'As Estátuas Fallando' (The Statues Speaking) featuring a literary dialogue where Justice appears as an animated statue character. 'A Verdade: Que dizes, ó Justiça?' (Truth: What do you

candidates.titulo: Estátua da Justiça (Luiz Rochet) — referência no Almanak Laemmert
candidates.justificativa: Reference in the Almanak Laemmert (1929 edition) to the statue of Justice by French sculptor Luiz Rochet, inaugurated September 1872. Alongside statues of History, Poetry and Science. Documents the im

candidates.titulo: A Justiça (Alfredo Ceschiatti, STF Brasília)
candidates.justificativa: Monumental granite sculpture of Justice (Themis) seated before the Supreme Federal Tribunal in Brasília. Eyes closed, holding a sword with both hands. Designed to complement Oscar Niemeyer's modernist

candidates.titulo: Eliseu Visconti — Alegoria (Biblioteca Nacional, Rio de Janeiro)
candidates.justificativa: Impressionist-style allegorical panel for the Presidential Room of the Biblioteca Nacional in Rio de Janeiro. Female allegorical figure in Visconti's distinctive impressionist idiom — the French-train

candidates.titulo: Alegoria da República (Estados Unidos do Brasil)
candidates.justificativa: Seated female allegory of the Brazilian Republic enthroned. Wears white classical dress with green mantle (national colors), laurel crown with red Phrygian cap, golden necklace. Right hand rests on a

candidates.titulo: 2000 Réis — Efígie da República com Barrete Frígio
candidates.justificativa: Bust of the Republic wearing Phrygian cap, profile facing left. Inscription: REPUBLICA DOS ESTADOS UNIDOS DO BRAZIL. Reverse: denomination and date within wreath. Positivist Republic period. Transplan

candidates.titulo: Gerechtigkeitsbrunnen mit Plastik der Justitia (Hans Gieng, 1543)
candidates.justificativa: The Gerechtigkeitsbrunnen (Fountain of Justice) in Bern — the first known blindfolded representation of Lady Justice (1543). Hans Gieng's statue holds sword and scales, blindfolded, standing atop vanq

candidates.titulo: Justitia (marble head from Porta di Capua)
candidates.justificativa: Marble head of Justitia from the Porta di Capua, built under Emperor Frederick II. One of the earliest surviving medieval personifications of Justice as a woman — predating the Renaissance tradition.

candidates.titulo: Die Göttin der Gerechtigkeit nach dem heutigen Geschmacke
candidates.justificativa: 'The Goddess of Justice according to contemporary taste' — an Enlightenment-era satirical engraving critiquing contemporary justice through its female allegorical form. Shows how the figure of Justice

candidates.titulo: Justitia an der Rathausuhr (Esslingen)
candidates.justificativa: Justitia figure on the town hall clock of Esslingen am Neckar (1589). Justice as a permanent civic presence on a public timepiece — marking the hours under the gaze of the female allegory of law.

candidates.titulo: Barocke Holzdecke: Personifikation der Gerechtigkeit
candidates.justificativa: Baroque wooden ceiling in a Lüneburg residential building with allegorical medallions: Personification of Justice. The female justice figure domesticated — appearing not only in courts and public buil

candidates.titulo: Allegorie: Justitia (Marcantonio Raimondi)
candidates.justificativa: Engraving of Justitia by Marcantonio Raimondi, the most important Italian engraver of the High Renaissance (pupil of Raphael's circle). This print disseminated the Italian Renaissance model of female

candidates.titulo: Allegorie der Justitia und eine weitere weibliche Allegorie
candidates.justificativa: Italian drawing or print showing Justitia alongside another female allegorical figure. Early 16th-century example of the pairing of Justice with other virtues (Prudence, Temperance, Fortitude) — the f

candidates.titulo: Justitia (Hameln, Stiftsherrenhaus)
candidates.justificativa: Justitia figure on the Stiftsherrenhaus (Canon's House) in Hamelin, c. 1558. A Weser Renaissance example of Justice as architectural ornament on a residential/ecclesiastical building.

candidates.titulo: Relief: Gerechtigkeit und Eintracht (Bremen Rathaus)
candidates.justificativa: Relief of Justice (Gerechtigkeit) and Concord (Eintracht) on the Bremen Old Town Hall (1596–1605). Female Justice paired with female Concord on a civic building — the visual argument that justice prod

candidates.titulo: Allegorie der Jus Civile (Christian Wenzinger)
candidates.justificativa: Allegory of Civil Law (Jus Civile) by Christian Wenzinger, 1752, at a former Benedictine abbey. Distinction between civil and canonical law personified — Justice in her specifically secular legal form

candidates.titulo: Kamin mit Justitia (Leipzig, Altes Rathaus)
candidates.justificativa: Fireplace with Justitia in the Leipzig Old Town Hall (c. 1610). Justice as a decorative element on a fireplace in the main council chamber — the warming presence of legal authority in the deliberative

candidates.titulo: Gerechtigkeit (Allegory of Justice) by Lucas Cranach the Elder
candidates.justificativa: Painting showing the personification of Justice as a half-length nude female figure against dark background, holding sword and scales. Inscribed 'GERECHTIGKAEIT'. One of the most influential Renaissan

candidates.titulo: Germania
candidates.justificativa: Personificação da Alemanha como mulher guerreira (Germania), pintada por Friedrich August von Kaulbach em agosto de 1914, no início da Primeira Guerra Mundial. A figura feminina erguida aparece com co

candidates.titulo: Helft uns siegen! Zeichnet die Kriegsanleihe
candidates.justificativa: Um dos cartazes de empréstimo de guerra alemães mais icônicos. Parte da série Kriegsanleihe que financiou o esforço bélico alemão. A campanha empregou sistematicamente figuras alegóricas femininas da

candidates.titulo: Germania — Guter Rat ist teuer (Bismarck e Alsácia-Lorena)
candidates.justificativa: Cartoon satírico do Kladderadatsch: Bismarck conduz as figuras femininas alegóricas de Alsácia e Lorena — jovens em trajes regionais — para o Império Alemão. Germania preside a absorção territorial.

candidates.titulo: Notgeld Bielefeld — Jungbrunnen (Fountain of Youth) silk note
50 Mark Reichsbanknote — Germania as Young Girl (Weimar 1919)
50 Reichspfennig — Woman Planting Oak Sapling (Weimar 1924)
Germania — Reichspost / Deutsches Reich Definitive Stamp (Paul Eduard Waldraff)
candidates.justificativa: Bust of Germania wearing octagonal imperial crown, holding sword and olive branch. Art Nouveau design personally chosen by Kaiser Wilhelm II. Model: actress Anna Führing. Longest-running German defini

candidates.titulo: Germania "Belgien" Overprint — Military Occupation Stamp (WWI)
candidates.justificativa: Germania definitive stamp overprinted with "Belgien" and revalued in Centimes/Francs for use in German-occupied Belgium during WWI. Same image as DE-GERM-1900 but with additional state inscription mar

candidates.titulo: [Alegoría de la República española y la República francesa]
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical drawing or print depicting the Spanish Republic and the French Republic as paired female figures. Produced in the context of the proclamation of the Second Spanish Republic (April 14, 1931

candidates.titulo: Alegoría de la Primera República española (La Flaca)
candidates.justificativa: Ilustração satírica publicada na revista republicana La Flaca (Barcelona, 1869–1873), em período correspondente à Primeira República espanhola (fev. 1873 – dez. 1874). Tomás Padró i Pedret, ilustrador

candidates.titulo: 5 Pesetas "Hispania Reclinada" — Gobierno Provisional
candidates.justificativa: Hispania reclinada coroada com coroa mural, apoiada nos Pireneus com Gibraltar a seus pés, segurando ramo de oliveira. Mar e raios de sol ao fundo. Moeda fundacional da Espanha pós-monárquica, cunhada

candidates.titulo: 1 Peseta "Hispania Sentada" — II República
candidates.justificativa: Hispania sentada sobre plinto, segurando ramo de oliveira. Última peseta de prata jamais cunhada. A continuidade da pose sentada de 1870 a 1933 revela a *Nachleben* da mesma *Pathosformel* através de

candidates.titulo: Thronende Justitia / Allegorie der strafenden Gerechtigkeit
candidates.justificativa: Photograph of a ceiling fresco in Koblenz showing Justitia enthroned — the allegorical female figure of criminal justice. A German example of Justice as a seated female sovereign figure, wielding puni

candidates.titulo: Time and Justice Reveal the Truth
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical depiction showing Time and Justice revealing Truth. Belgian example of the Justice-Truth triad where Justice is personified as a woman who, together with Time, unveils Truth — a visual arg

candidates.titulo: Justitia (Jacob Matham)
candidates.justificativa: Print depicting Justitia — the female allegorical figure of Justice with her traditional attributes (scales, sword). By Jacob Matham, a major Dutch engraver, student of Hendrick Goltzius.

candidates.titulo: Allegory of Justice (Charles Audran after B.P. Cortona)
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical representation of Justice, engraved by Charles Audran after an invention by Pietro da Cortona (Berrettini). Italian Baroque model of Justice as a female figure — one of the visual template

candidates.titulo: Justitia (Hans Kels d. J.)
candidates.justificativa: Golden gilded relief sculpture depicting Justitia. A Renaissance example of the female allegorical figure of justice rendered in precious materials — connecting legal symbolism to material luxury and

candidates.titulo: Justitia, Allegory of Justice (Johann Oswald Harms)
candidates.justificativa: Drawing depicting Justitia — allegorical female figure of justice with scales and sword. German Baroque representation of the legal feminine allegory.

candidates.titulo: Justitia civilis (Maarten de Vos)
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical depiction of civil justice (Justitia civilis) by Flemish master Maarten de Vos. Distinction between civil and criminal justice personified as separate female figures — important for unders

candidates.titulo: Allegory with Justizia, crowned by a putto
candidates.justificativa: Allegorical representation of Justizia resting on clouds, being crowned by a putto (cherub), with scales visible. Justice as a celestial female figure receiving divine coronation — the theological dim

candidates.titulo: Female Allegory (Pierre Puvis de Chavannes)
candidates.justificativa: Female allegorical figure by Pierre Puvis de Chavannes, the great French muralist whose public commissions (Sorbonne, Panthéon, Hôtel de Ville) defined the visual language of Third Republic civic spac

candidates.titulo: La Justice, la Vengeance et la Vérité
candidates.justificativa: Engraving depicting three female allegorical figures: Justice, Vengeance, and Truth. A powerful composition showing Justice as a dominant female presence, flanked by Truth and divine Vengeance — key t

candidates.titulo: Le triomphe de la Justice et de la Vérité
candidates.justificativa: Engraving showing the triumph of Justice and Truth as female allegorical figures. Complete state with full lettering. Part of Chifflart's series on juridical allegory.

candidates.titulo: L'envoyé de la Justice
candidates.justificativa: Engraving titled 'The Envoy of Justice' — depicting a figure sent by Justice (as a female allegorical power). Early example in the series of Justice representations by Chifflart.

candidates.titulo: Marianne enseignant l'alphabet à des enfants (naïades et Marianne)
candidates.justificativa: Engravings depicting naiads and Marianne teaching the alphabet to children. Powerful image of the Republic-as-woman as educator — embodying the republican ideal of laïcité and public instruction throu

candidates.titulo: La République aimable (Félicien Rops)
candidates.justificativa: Satirical engraving by Belgian-French artist Félicien Rops depicting 'The Amiable Republic' — a subversive or eroticized representation of the Republic as a woman. Critical for understanding the conte

candidates.titulo: The French Crisis: Madame La République
candidates.justificativa: Political cartoon from 1887 showing 'Madame La République' during the French political crisis. The Republic personified as a woman under stress — a key document of how female allegory was deployed dur

candidates.titulo: République Française: 3e Emprunt de la Défense nationale
candidates.justificativa: WWI propaganda poster for the 3rd National Defense loan. Marianne stands triumphantly above soldiers, embodying the Republic rallying her citizens to war. The female allegory mobilized for military pr

candidates.titulo: La République nous appelle... (Steinlen)
candidates.justificativa: WWI recruitment poster: 'The Republic calls us...' — Marianne as a summoning female figure, calling citizens to arms. Steinlen's powerful composition shows the Republic-as-woman as a quasi-maternal/mi

candidates.titulo: Buste de la République (Agence Rol)
candidates.justificativa: Press photograph of a bust sculpture representing the French Republic. Busts of Marianne became ubiquitous in French town halls (mairies) under the Third Republic — the multiplication of the female al

candidates.titulo: Le hochet de la République (Jean Veber)
candidates.justificativa: Satirical print 'The Rattle of the Republic' by Jean Veber. The Republic as a woman reduced to a toy or rattle — a critical/ironic commentary on the infantilization or trivialization of republican sym

candidates.titulo: Illustrations de Calendrier et compost des bergiers (Danse macabre — female Justice figure)
candidates.justificativa: Illustrations from the 'Calendar and Almanac of Shepherds' featuring the dance of death and allegorical figures including Justice. An early representation of Justice as a female figure within the meme

candidates.titulo: La Liberté guidant le peuple (Eugène Delacroix)
candidates.justificativa: 'Liberty Leading the People' — the iconic image of the July Revolution of 1830. A bare-breasted female figure (Liberty/Marianne) leads armed citizens over barricades, holding the tricolour flag and a

candidates.titulo: Déclaration des droits de l'homme et du citoyen
candidates.justificativa: Engraved broadside of the Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen (1789), designed by Le Barbier l'aîné and engraved by Laurent and Dien. The composition frames the text of the Declaration with t

candidates.titulo: Déclaration des droits de l'homme et du citoyen (variante)
candidates.justificativa: Anonymous engraved broadside variant of the Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen (1789). Presents the text of the Declaration within an allegorical decorative framework featuring female figure

candidates.titulo: Allégorie pour le frontispice du compte-rendu au Roi par M. Necker
candidates.justificativa: Engraved allegorical frontispiece designed for Necker's 'Compte rendu au Roi' (1781), the public financial report that became a landmark in Ancien Régime transparency. Female allegorical figures — var

candidates.titulo: Allégorie pour le frontispice du compte rendu au Roi par M. Necker (variante)
candidates.justificativa: Second variant of the allegorical engraving designed as frontispiece for Necker's 'Compte rendu au Roi' (1781). Female allegorical figures in a variant compositional arrangement. Documents the multipl

candidates.titulo: Allégorie pour servir de frontispice au compte-rendu au Roi par M. Necker
candidates.justificativa: Third allegorical engraving serving as frontispiece for Necker's 'Compte rendu au Roi' (1781). This variant reinforces the corpus of female allegorical imagery mobilized around the question of royal f

candidates.titulo: [Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité — portrait allégorique d'Ernouf]
candidates.justificativa: Revolutionary allegorical print combining the motto Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité with a portrait of Ernouf. Designed by Garneray and engraved by Quéverdo, this composite image exemplifies the visual c

candidates.titulo: Journée du Poilu, 25 et 26 décembre 1915, organisée par le Parlement
candidates.justificativa: WWI charity poster by Steinlen (1915) for the 'Journée du Poilu' (Soldier's Day, December 25–26), organized by the French Parliament to raise funds for soldiers at the front. The composition depicts a

candidates.titulo: Decretum Gratiani (manuscrito iluminado, BnF)
candidates.justificativa: Manuscrito iluminado do Decretum Gratiani (Concordia discordantium canonum, c. 1140), coletânea de direito canônico compilada pelo monge Graciano em Bolonha, conservado na Bibliothèque nationale de Fr

candidates.titulo: La Liberté guidant le peuple
candidates.justificativa: Pintura icônica de Delacroix representando a Liberdade como mulher com barrete frígio, seio nu, empunhando a bandeira tricolor e um mosquete, guiando o povo sobre barricadas durante a Revolução de Jul

candidates.titulo: Notre-Dame des colonies
candidates.justificativa: Litografia satírica de Jean Veber representando uma Marianne como "Nossa Senhora das Colônias" — paródia da iconografia religiosa e republicana a serviço da crítica ao imperialismo colonial francês. A

candidates.titulo: Bons de la Défense nationale
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz de propaganda para bônus de defesa nacional durante a Primeira Guerra Mundial. Marianne aparece como figura convocatória, associada a símbolos patrióticos e militares, incentivando a subscrição

candidates.titulo: 3e Emprunt de la Défense nationale (République française)
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz do 3o Empréstimo de Defesa Nacional da República Francesa (1917). Marianne é representada em posição hierática, investida de autoridade marcial, acima de cenas de mobilização militar. Instrumen

candidates.titulo: Emprunt national 1920 (Société générale)
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz do Empréstimo Nacional de 1920, patrocinado pela Société Générale. Marianne aparece como símbolo de reconstrução nacional no imediato pós-guerra, associada à estabilidade financeira e à continu

candidates.titulo: Emprunt national 1920 (Banque Nationale de Paris et des Pays-Bas)
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz do Empréstimo Nacional de 1920, patrocinado pela Banque Nationale de Paris et des Pays-Bas. Variante institucional-bancária do cartaz de empréstimo, com Marianne em registro solene e institucio

candidates.titulo: Emprunt de la Libération (Rente française 1918)
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz do Empréstimo da Libertação (1918), marcando a transição guerra–paz. Marianne/Liberté é invocada simultaneamente como símbolo de vitória militar e de reconstrução, em registro entre o MILITAR e

candidates.titulo: Prêtez à la France pour qu'elle grandisse dans le rayonnement de la Paix!
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz do Comptoir National d'Escompte de Paris (1920). "Empreste à França para que ela cresça no esplendor da Paz!" — Marianne é associada à Paz e ao crescimento nacional, num registro pós-bélico de

candidates.titulo: Emprunt national 1920 (Société centrale des banques de province)
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz do Empréstimo Nacional de 1920, emitido pela Société centrale des banques de province. Distribuição provincial do programa de empréstimos nacionais, com Marianne como signo de unidade nacional.

candidates.titulo: En avant! Armée de l'Épargne, c'est pour la Patrie
candidates.justificativa: Cartaz de propaganda "Em frente! Exército da Poupança, é pela Pátria" (1916). Linguagem militarizada aplicada à economia de guerra — a poupança é apresentada como forma de serviço militar, com Mariann

candidates.titulo: Madame Anastasie (avec les ciseaux de la censure)
candidates.justificativa: Capa de L'Éclipse no. 209: velha quase surda e cega agarrando tesouras enormes — personificação da censura da imprensa. Encarna o aparato repressivo do governo da Ordem Moral sob MacMahon. A "Anastasi

candidates.titulo: La Paix — Idylle
candidates.justificativa: Litografia da série Actualités: paisagem devastada de ruínas após a Guerra Franco-Prussiana. O título irônico "Paz — Idílio" transforma o gênero pastoral/feminino do idílio em cena de destruição total

candidates.titulo: L'Empire c'est la paix
candidates.justificativa: Litografia da série Actualités: campo de ruínas e ossos com inscrição sarcástica citando o motto inaugural de Napoleão III. A figura feminina alegórica da França/Paz está conspicuamente ausente da pai

candidates.titulo: Calendrier républicain (An III)
candidates.justificativa: Republican calendar (Year III) in two engraved panels. Upper register features female allegorical figures flanking a central medallion with fasces and Phrygian cap. Left panel: Liberty and Equality wi

candidates.titulo: Liberté (d'après Moitte)
candidates.justificativa: Seated female Liberty holding a Phrygian cap aloft on a pole in her right hand. Classical drapery, laurel crown. Seated on a stone pedestal inscribed 'LIBERTÉ'. Left arm rests on fasces bound with rib

candidates.titulo: La Liberté, soutenue par la Raison, protège l'Innocence et couronne la Vertu
candidates.justificativa: Multi-figure allegorical composition. Winged Liberty at left crowns Virtue (seated right, holding radiant mirror/sun). Reason at center steadies the scene. Innocence crouches at Virtue's feet. Lion at

candidates.titulo: La Liberté armée du Sceptre de la Raison foudroye l'Ignorance et le Fanatisme
candidates.justificativa: Companion piece to FR-039. Liberty seated at center, flanked by standing Reason at left (with lion). Reason wields a radiant scepter/sun-disk emitting lightning bolts that strike Ignorance and Fanatic

candidates.titulo: Iustitia (d'après Bruegel)
candidates.justificativa: Panoramic allegorical engraving of Justice (Iustitia) from Bruegel's Seven Virtues series. Central female figure of Justice stands blindfolded with sword and scales, surrounded by scenes of juridical

candidates.titulo: Semeuse 1 Franc silver coin (Oscar Roty)
Piastre de Commerce — Marianne Assise avec Fasces
candidates.justificativa: Marianne seated left on throne holding fasces, wearing radiate crown. Wheat bundles to left, anchor to right. Reverse: INDO-CHINE FRANÇAISE. Colonial trade coin for Indochina.

candidates.titulo: 5 Francs Hercule — La République et La Justice flanquant Hercule
candidates.justificativa: Hercules standing center flanked by two female allegories: Republic (left, with pike topped by Phrygian cap) and Justice/Equality (right, with triangular level). Design by Dupré (1795), reissued 1848

candidates.titulo: Semeuse — Selo Definitivo da República Francesa (Oscar Roty / Mouchon)
candidates.justificativa: Marianne in flowing robes and Phrygian cap sowing seeds with extended right arm, seed bag under left arm. Rising sun on horizon (1903 version). Design from Roty medal (1887), adapted for coin (1897) t

candidates.titulo: Sottoscrivete al Prestito — Italia Turrita
candidates.justificativa: Italia Turrita com coroa mural, armadura e tricolor italiano, segurando espada apontada contra guerreiro nórdico/germânico avançando sobre montanhas. Publicado em Bérgamo pelo Istituto Italiano d'Arti

candidates.titulo: 100 Lire "Minerva" — Testa d'Italia laureata
candidates.justificativa: Anverso: cabeça feminina da Italia coroada de louros, virada à esquerda, inspirada em moedas siracusanas (séc. IV a.C.). Reverso: Minerva de pé com lança e loureiro. A Italia laureada é variante secul

candidates.titulo: 500 Lire "Caravelle" — Italia Turrita
candidates.justificativa: Anverso: busto de mulher renascentista com coroa mural (Italia Turrita), cercada por 19 brasões de capitais regionais. Reverso: três caravelas. Inscrição: REPVBBLICA ITALIANA. 97,8M de exemplares.

candidates.titulo: Selo "Siracusana" — Italia Turrita
candidates.justificativa: Série definitiva mais longeva da República Italiana. Cabeça feminina com coroa de torres e muralhas (corona muralis), inspirada em moeda da antiga Siracusa. Emitida em múltiplas denominações.

candidates.titulo: [Virtudes cardinales: Justica] (La Patria Ilustrada)
candidates.justificativa: Litografia de José Guadalupe Posada publicada na revista mexicana La Patria Ilustrada (Cidade do México, 18 fev. 1889), integrante da série 'Virtudes cardinales' (Virtudes cardinais). A imagem represe

candidates.titulo: Alegoría de la República Mexicana con Símbolos Masónicos
candidates.justificativa: Miniatura em aquarela sobre marfim. República Mexicana personificada como mulher de azul com bordados dourados, cocar de plumas e coroa de ouro, segurando malho. Acima, triângulo radiante com olho (sí

candidates.titulo: Vrouwe Justitia (nude, with sword and scales)

candidates.titulo: Wake Up America! Civilization Calls Every Man, Woman and Child!

theoretical_contributions.name: Anti-ENDURECIMENTO
theoretical_contributions.desc: Regime collapse reverses purification. Three paths: phantom persistence, satirical reversal, weaponization.

theoretical_contributions.name: ENDURECIMENTO negativo
theoretical_contributions.desc: Hardening by subtraction (post-1945 Marianne removes body, cap, movement).

theoretical_contributions.name: Captura transnacional
theoretical_contributions.desc: Enemy seizes and inverts your allegory (Goetz capturing Marianne).

theoretical_contributions.name: Dissolucao heraldica
theoretical_contributions.desc: ENDURECIMENTO terminus where the female body disappears entirely into heraldic residue (Belgian coins).

theoretical_contributions.name: Principio da redundancia
theoretical_contributions.desc: Where the courthouse exists in stone, Justitia vanishes from money (Notgeld tribunal cities).

theoretical_contributions.name: Indigenizacao
theoretical_contributions.desc: Colonial alternative to ENDURECIMENTO: replace European allegorical body with racialized indigenous body (French colonial stamps).

theoretical_contributions.name: Gradiente de transparencia da violencia imperial
theoretical_contributions.desc: Olive branch (US) -> fasces (FR) -> trident (UK). Three levels of honesty about what the female body sanctions.

theoretical_contributions.name: Ausencia pre-modelo vs. pos-modelo
theoretical_contributions.desc: Two types of absent female allegory: Mexico (1824, pre-model — did not know the European pattern) vs. Japan (1875, post-model — knew and consciously rejected). Japan copied metal, weight, fineness and English inscriptions but rejected the female body. Strongest cultural delimitation argument.

theoretical_contributions.name: ENDURECIMENTO sinconico e comparativo
theoretical_contributions.desc: ENDURECIMENTO is not only diachronic: UK militarizes by addition (Britannia armed, seahorse chariot), France pastoralaizes by subtraction (Semeuse removes political attributes) — two synchronic modes of neutralizing the female allegorical body (1903-1913).

theoretical_contributions.name: Numen mixtum
theoretical_contributions.desc: Deliberate iconographic fusion of two deities into a single normalized legal type (Themis + Minerva in Belgian court sculpture, documented by Huygebaert 2019). Maps onto purification indicators uniformizacao_facial and apagamento_narrativo.
